Picea glauca, also known as the white spruce, is a coniferous tree species native to eastern Canada and the northeastern United States. The health benefits of Picea glauca include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the severity of respiratory conditions such as asthma and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Picea glauca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Picea glauca include essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
